sections.title: Attempting to climb down the nearby ladder

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Hey! You don't have permission to go down there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Hello there. Welcome to the Temple of the Lesser Gods.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about the lesser gods?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Of course. The lesser gods, or avatars as some call them, were born from the dreams of Tumeken. They are Apmeken, Het, Crondis and Scabaras.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about Apmeken?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Apmeken is the goddess of companionship. She is known to be the most mischievous, playful and unpredictable of the minor deities. I'd say she's my personal favourite.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I shouldn't trust you then?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Apmeken can appeal to the most erudite scholar or the most barefaced liar. I'd like to be considered amongst the former category.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. What does she look like?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: She has the head of a monkey, or sometimes an ape. It changes depending upon what nature she is displaying. For example, the orangutan represents wisdom, while the baboon represents mischief.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about Het?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Het is the least strange of our deities to foreign eyes, for he looks just like us. I suspect this is why his shrines are particularly targeted by thieves like that infamous Templeton.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does he represent?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Het is the god of health, both in mind and body. He is quite popular amongst soldiers as a result, especially up in Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just soldiers?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Not at all! Like I said, the wellbeing of the mind is also key to Het. Many great orators also look to him for guidance. I imagine his teachings help with some of their strenuous speeches.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did his followers react to the plagues here?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: With many arguments. They really couldn't decide whether it was a punishment from Het, or an affront to him. I stayed out of that debate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about Crondis?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Crondis is a ferocious lady with the head of a mighty crocodile.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That must be awkward when buying helmets.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Luckily for her, I don't think deities have much need for them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what is she the goddess of?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Resourcefulness. A word which seems to be open to large interpretation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Most consider her to be a teacher of modesty. They say we should be resourceful by only taking and using that which we absolutely need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But there's others with a different interpretation?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Some believe she is actually the goddess of pleasure. They claim that the idea of modesty came from a misinterpretation, and that to be resourceful is to take all that you can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't sound right.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: No. It is a more recent take that I suspect was born in Menaphos. There are many in that city with more wealth than they'll ever need. Modesty is not an ideal they'll ever embody.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about Scabaras?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: There are many opinions out there on Scabaras, but general knowledge isn't always the most accurate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well what are the absolute facts then?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Scabaras is the god of isolation. He takes on the appearance of a man with the head of a scarab. Both he and his followers were driven into hiding long ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: And that's it. You asked for the absolute facts. Anything beyond that is just speculation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But there must be more. The High Priest told me that Scabaras tried to conquer these lands.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: So it is said, but the written accounts that exist contain many inconsistencies when it comes to what role Scabaras actually played. Of course, most would not know that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Many of the writings are only available to us priests. That does of course include the new High Priest, but perhaps he is yet to read them. They are definitely... illuminating.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.
